parsed.clarification_question: Should the look focus mainly on writing polish, or on technical validity and overclaiming?

rationale.R1.2: An open-ended look at this research paragraph centrally includes checking whether the theoretical reasoning and conclusions cohere.
rationale.R1.3: The paragraph makes empirical claims tied to reported Reynolds-number experiments and error values, so claim-evidence alignment is central.
rationale.R1.6: Because the delegation refers to a paragraph, sentence- and paragraph-level readability and register are central expected responsibilities.
rationale.RX.1: Any projection should preserve uncertainty because the delegation does not specify the intended kind of feedback.
rationale.RX.2: The artifact contains broad claims about turbulence, uniqueness, generalization, and solver alternatives, making overclaim avoidance active.
rationale.RX.3: The agent must only project responsibility and not perform the requested review or rewrite.
rationale.RX.4: Technical claims about simulation methods and turbulence should avoid downstream misuse from overstated validity.
rationale.RX.5: The projection should remain traceable to the delegation wording and the artifact’s explicit claims.
